collages.title: Am Strand
collages.description: An exploration of oceanic memory and the geography of forgetting. Found paper ephemera, vintage atlas pages, and cyanotype fragments layered into a meditation on cartographic desire.
collages.materials: Archival paper, cyanotype prints, vintage atlas pages, acrylic medium

collages.title: Blatt
collages.description: Fragments of domesticity reassembled into something stranger. Old recipe cards, wallpaper samples, and botanical illustration offcuts form a taxonomy of the intimate everyday.
collages.materials: Recipe cards, wallpaper samples, botanical prints, wax

collages.title: Gustav Fischer
collages.description: The first in a series examining motion as memory trace. Chronophotographic influence combined with contemporary print media creates a visual stutter — the image caught between moments.
collages.materials: Newspaper, acetate, photographic paper, spray paint

collages.title: The Herbalist's Index
collages.description: A false taxonomy of healing plants that never existed. Combining real medicinal woodcuts with invented Latin nomenclature and hand-lettered field notes.
collages.materials: Woodcut reproductions, handmade paper, iron gall ink, pressed plant matter

collages.title: Obernetter
collages.description: Cities dissolve into geometry after dark. Sourced from a collection of 1970s architecture magazines, this piece isolates the structural skeleton from its human context.
collages.materials: Architecture magazine pages, black gesso, gold leaf, linen

collages.title: Plate 15
collages.description: Found portraits of unknown women combined with the technical diagrams of early aircraft — a feminist archaeology of erased histories. Who flew? Who was allowed to dream of flying?
collages.materials: Vintage portrait photography, aircraft technical diagrams, tissue paper, shellac

collages.title: This and That
collages.description: Found portraits of unknown women combined with the technical diagrams of early aircraft — a feminist archaeology of erased histories. Who flew? Who was allowed to dream of flying?
collages.materials: Vintage portrait photography, aircraft technical diagrams, tissue paper, shellac
